ALG:
## Read
Vladimir Petković rehabilitated Algeria after a tough opener and carried the Fennecs to the Round of 32 as one of the best third-place finishers in Group J.
## Campaign
Lost to Argentina 3-0 but bounced back: a 2-1 comeback win over Jordan and an electrifying 3-3 draw with Austria, with two goals from Mahrez, to seal the spot. GS: 1W 1D 1L. Faces Switzerland in the Round of 32.
ARG:
## Read
Lionel Scaloni keeps the world-champion core and the favorite's tag. Argentina went 100% in the Group Stage, with an unstoppable Messi and a nearly impenetrable defense, and awaits Cape Verde in the Round of 32.
## Campaign
Three wins in Group J: 3-0 over Algeria (Messi hat-trick), 2-0 over Austria and 3-1 over Jordan. Eight goals scored, only one conceded — the mature soccer of a title contender, with a well-rotated squad and command of possession.
AUS:
## Read
Tony Popovic built a solid, organized Australia that advanced to the Round of 16 in 2nd place in Group D with 4 points.
## Campaign
Opened by beating Turkey 2-0, lost to the United States 2-0 and held the 0-0 with Paraguay that was enough to qualify. A disciplined defense: only 2 goals conceded.
AUT:
## Read
Ralf Rangnick's trademark intensity carried Austria to 2nd place in Group J and the Round of 32.
## Campaign
3-1 over Jordan, a 2-0 loss to Argentina and a dramatic 3-3 draw with Algeria, with Kalajdzic saving the point in the 90+6'. Up next, a marquee test: Spain.
BEL:
## Read
Rudi Garcia struggled at the start but led Belgium to the top of Group G.
## Campaign
A lukewarm beginning with a 1-1 against Egypt and a 0-0 with Iran (down a man), before the explosion: 5-1 over New Zealand, with two from Trossard. First place clinched on goal difference.
BIH:
## Read
Sergej Barbarez took Bosnia to the knockout rounds as one of the eight best third-place finishers, leaning on the experience of Edin Džeko.
## Campaign
A 1-1 draw with Canada, a 4-1 loss to Switzerland and a 3-1 win over Qatar that kept the 4 points competitive. Faces the United States in the Round of 32.
BRA:
## Read
Carlo Ancelotti leads Brazil with the calm of a man who has won it all. Top of Group C with the best defense of the first round, the Seleção needed the Italian's experience to come from behind against Japan in the Round of 32 and reach the Round of 16, where it faces Norway.
## Campaign
In the Group Stage, 2 wins (3-0 over Haiti and Scotland) and a 1-1 draw with Morocco in the opener. In the Round of 32, fell behind against Japan (Sano, 29') and won 2-1 in a comeback, with Casemiro and Gabriel Martinelli in stoppage time. Attack led by Vinícius Jr and Matheus Cunha.
CAN:
## Read
Jesse Marsch has the host nation in the Round of 16. Canada edged South Africa 1-0 in the Round of 32, with Eustáquio in stoppage time, and advanced with a scare.
## Campaign
In the Group Stage, a draw with Bosnia, 6-0 over Qatar (Jonathan David hat-trick) and a loss to Switzerland. In the Round of 32, the decision came on the final play. Now faces Morocco in the Round of 16.
CIV:
## Read
Emerse Faé made history — Ivory Coast reached the Round of 32 for the first time — but the campaign ended there, eliminated by Norway.
## Campaign
2nd in Group E with wins over Ecuador and Curaçao and a loss to Germany. In the Round of 32, lost 2-1 to Norway, with Amad Diallo pulling one back. An exit with heads held high.
COD:
## Read
Sébastien Desabre made history with DR Congo: a first-ever knockout-round berth as one of the best third-place finishers in Group K.
## Campaign
A deserved 1-1 draw with Portugal, a loss to Colombia and a dramatic 3-1 comeback win over Uzbekistan, with two from Yoane Wissa (3 goals in the World Cup). The Leopards in the Round of 32.
COL:
## Read
Néstor Lorenzo completed the mission with authority: Colombia top of Group K and impressive at the back, with only one goal conceded.
## Campaign
3-1 over Uzbekistan, 1-0 over DR Congo (Daniel Muñoz decisive) and 0-0 with Portugal, which secured top spot. Luis Díaz's generation arrives at the knockout rounds in form.
CPV:
## Read
Bubista put Cape Verde on the map: a first-ever knockout-round berth, in 2nd place in Group H, unbeaten.
## Campaign
Three very disciplined draws — 0-0 with Spain, 2-2 with Uruguay and 0-0 with Saudi Arabia. Defensive solidity was the hallmark of the Blue Sharks.
CRO:
## Read
Zlatko Dalić adjusted Croatia after a poor opener and secured 2nd place in Group L.
## Campaign
A 4-2 loss to England, followed by hard-fought wins over Panama (1-0) and Ghana (2-1) in the decider for the spot. Up next, Portugal in the Round of 32.
CUW:
## Read
Veteran Dick Advocaat saw Curaçao off from its first World Cup in the Group Stage, but with a historic point on the résumé.
## Campaign
Took a 7-1 from Germany but held a heroic 0-0 with Ecuador — the country's first-ever point at World Cups — before the 2-0 loss to Ivory Coast. Left with heads held high.
CZE:
## Read
Miroslav Koubek watched the Czech Republic sink to the bottom of Group A, eliminated without a win.
## Campaign
A 2-1 loss to South Korea, a 1-1 draw with South Africa and a 3-0 defeat to Mexico. The attack produced early, but the defense collapsed.
ECU:
## Read
Sebastián Beccacece took Ecuador to the knockout rounds as one of the best third-place finishers, with the most disciplined defense in the group: only 2 goals conceded.
## Campaign
A loss to Ivory Coast, 0-0 with Curaçao and the final blow — 2-1 over Germany, with Plata turning it around. Faces Mexico in the Round of 32.
EGY:
## Read
Hossam Hassan qualified Egypt in 2nd place in Group G, with Mohamed Salah as the reference point.
## Campaign
1-1 with Belgium, a 3-1 comeback win over New Zealand (Salah on the scoresheet) and 1-1 with Iran, which stamped the ticket. The Pharaohs into the knockout rounds on goal difference.
ENG:
## Read
Thomas Tuchel lived up to the favorite's billing: England top and unbeaten in Group L, with firepower and solidity.
## Campaign
4-2 over Croatia (two from Kane), 0-0 with Ghana and 2-0 over Panama. Two clean sheets and a luxury bench (Saka, Eze, Rashford). Faces DR Congo.
ESP:
## Read
Luis de la Fuente dominated Group H with the best defense in the section — no goals conceded in three matches.
## Campaign
0-0 with Cape Verde, 4-0 over Saudi Arabia (Yamal and Oyarzabal) and 1-0 over Uruguay. La Roja establishes itself as a title contender heading into the knockout rounds.
FRA:
## Read
Didier Deschamps closed out Group I with 100% and the best attack in the section, driven by Mbappé and Dembélé, with four goals each.
## Campaign
3-1 over Senegal, 3-0 over Iraq and 4-1 over Norway, with a Dembélé hat-trick. Runaway leader, faces Sweden in the Round of 32.
GER:
## Read
A melancholy elimination for Julian Nagelsmann: Germany topped Group E but fell in the Round of 32 to Paraguay on penalties — the World Cup's big upset.
## Campaign
7-1 over Curaçao, 2-1 over Ivory Coast and a loss to Ecuador. In the Round of 32, 1-1 with Paraguay and 4-3 on penalties. End of the line.
GHA:
## Read
Otto Addo took Ghana to the knockout rounds as one of the best third-place finishers, leaning on a disciplined defense.
## Campaign
1-0 over Panama, 0-0 with England and a 2-1 loss to Croatia. Faces Colombia in the Round of 32.
HAI:
## Read
Sébastien Migné bowed out of the World Cup with Haiti at the bottom of Group C, without a point.
## Campaign
Losses to Scotland (1-0), Brazil (3-0) and Morocco (4-2). Despite the effort, the defense conceded goals in every match.
IRN:
## Read
Amir Ghalenoei saw Iran eliminated by the finest margins — unbeaten, with three draws, but 9th among the third-place finishers, one spot below the cut.
## Campaign
2-2 with New Zealand, 0-0 with Belgium and 1-1 with Egypt. It lacked turning solidity into wins; Senegal took the spot on goal difference.
IRQ:
## Read
Graham Arnold endured a campaign to forget with Iraq, at the bottom of Group I.
## Campaign
3-0 to France, 4-1 to Norway and 5-0 to Senegal. Twelve goals conceded and no points. Eliminated.
JOR:
## Read
Jamal Sellami made Jordan's World Cup debut and, even eliminated in the Group Stage, left a mark: a goal in all three matches.
## Campaign
Losses to Austria (3-1), Algeria (2-1) and Argentina (3-1). A fragile defense, but the historic record of the country's first-ever goal at World Cups.
JPN:
## Read
Hajime Moriyasu came close to an upset: Japan went ahead of Brazil in the Round of 32 before conceding the comeback and falling 2-1.
## Campaign
Unbeaten in the Group Stage (2-2 with the Netherlands, 4-0 over Tunisia, 1-1 with Sweden), 2nd in Group F. Eliminated honorably by Brazil.
KOR:
## Read
Hong Myung-bo saw South Korea eliminated in the Group Stage — 3rd in Group A and 10th among the third-place finishers, outside the cut.
## Campaign
A 2-1 comeback win over the Czech Republic, then losses to Mexico (1-0) and South Africa (1-0). It lacked attacking sharpness in the decisive matches.
KSA:
## Read
Giorgos Donis bowed out of the World Cup with Saudi Arabia at the bottom of Group H, without a win.
## Campaign
The 4-0 loss to Spain in the opener weighed on the goal difference; then, 1-1 with Uruguay and 0-0 with Cape Verde. It lacked the firepower to dream of qualifying.
MAR:
## Read
Walid Regragui keeps Morocco alive and dreaming big: unbeaten, it advanced to the Round of 16 after eliminating the Netherlands on penalties in the Round of 32.
## Campaign
2nd in Group C with an inspired Saibari (1-1 with Brazil, 1-0 over Scotland, 4-2 over Haiti). In the Round of 32, 1-1 with the Netherlands and qualification 3-2 on penalties. Faces Canada in the Round of 16.
MEX:
## Read
Javier Aguirre has the host nation flying high: Mexico the outright leader of Group A, three wins and three clean sheets.
## Campaign
2-0 over South Africa, 1-0 over South Korea and 3-0 over the Czech Republic. An impenetrable defense and a clinical attack — the favorite's tag confirmed.
NED:
## Read
Ronald Koeman had a dominant Netherlands in the Group Stage, but the Oranje fell in the Round of 32 to Morocco on penalties.
## Campaign
Top of Group F with the best attack (10 goals): 2-2 with Japan, 5-1 over Sweden, 3-1 over Tunisia. In the Round of 32, 1-1 with Morocco and a 3-2 loss on penalties. End of the line.
NOR:
## Read
Ståle Solbakken took Haaland's Norway to the Round of 16. After stumbling in the opener, it responded and beat Ivory Coast 2-1 in the Round of 32.
## Campaign
A 4-1 loss to France, then 3-2 over Senegal and 4-1 over Iraq, with Haaland in top form. In the Round of 32, it got past Ivory Coast. Faces Brazil in the Round of 16.
NZL:
## Read
Darren Bazeley bowed out of the World Cup with New Zealand at the bottom of Group G.
## Campaign
3-1 to Egypt, a surprising 2-2 with Iran (two from Elijah Just) and 5-1 to Belgium. The point against Iran was the only one of the campaign.
PAN:
## Read
Thomas Christiansen bowed out of the World Cup with Panama without a point and without a goal, at the bottom of Group L.
## Campaign
Three narrow-margin losses — 1-0 to Ghana, 1-0 to Croatia and 2-0 to England. It lacked attacking punch.
PAR:
## Read
What a campaign from Gustavo Alfaro: qualified among the best third-place finishers, Paraguay made history by eliminating Germany on penalties and reaching the Round of 16.
## Campaign
3rd in Group D (loss to the USA, 1-0 over Turkey, 0-0 with Australia). In the Round of 32, 1-1 with Germany and 4-3 on penalties, with Gill saving two spot-kicks. Awaits the winner of France vs. Sweden.
POR:
## Read
Roberto Martínez qualified Portugal in 2nd place in Group K, alternating an overwhelming attack with two draws against direct rivals.
## Campaign
1-1 with DR Congo, 5-0 over Uzbekistan (two from Cristiano Ronaldo) and 0-0 with Colombia, which handed over top spot. A solid defense — only one goal conceded.
QAT:
## Read
Julen Lopetegui endured a Group Stage to forget with Qatar at the bottom of Group B, eliminated.
## Campaign
After a promising 1-1 with Switzerland, it collapsed — 6-0 to Canada (with two red cards) and 3-1 to Bosnia. Ten goals conceded and plenty of indiscipline.
RSA:
## Read
Hugo Broos put together a courageous campaign, but South Africa stopped in the Round of 32.
## Campaign
Surprised by qualifying in 2nd place in Group A, with a win over South Korea in the head-to-head. In the Round of 32, it held Canada for nearly the whole match and only gave in during stoppage time, eliminated 1-0. Among the 32 best.
SCO:
## Read
Steve Clarke saw Scotland eliminated — 3rd in Group C and 11th among the third-place finishers, outside the cut.
## Campaign
A promising opener with a 1-0 over Haiti (McGinn), but then 1-0 to Morocco and 3-0 to Brazil. It lacked attacking punch: only one goal in three matches.
SEN:
## Read
Pape Thiaw suffered more than expected but secured Senegal in the knockout rounds among the best third-place finishers.
## Campaign
Losses to France (3-1) and Norway (3-2, two from Sarr), before the 5-0 rout of Iraq that put the team back in the qualifying zone on goal difference. Faces Belgium.
SUI:
## Read
Murat Yakin built a solid Switzerland, the unbeaten leader of Group B.
## Campaign
After the 1-1 with Qatar, it found its rhythm — 4-1 over Bosnia and 2-1 over Canada in the decider for top spot. With Xhaka, Embolo and the breakout Manzambi, it advances in form.
SWE:
## Read
Graham Potter took Sweden to the knockout rounds as one of the best third-place finishers, with Isak and Gyökeres up front.
## Campaign
5-1 over Tunisia, a 5-1 loss to the Netherlands and 1-1 with Japan. It wavered at the back, but the goal difference from the opening rout secured the spot. Faces France.
TUN:
## Read
Hervé Renard endured a campaign to forget with Tunisia at the bottom of Group F, with the worst goal difference of the Group Stage.
## Campaign
5-1 to Sweden, 4-0 to Japan and 3-1 to the Netherlands. Twelve goals conceded — the fragile defense was the big problem.
TUR:
## Read
Vincenzo Montella was at the center of a disappointment: Turkey went out in the Group Stage even with Arda Güler and Çalhanoğlu.
## Campaign
Scoreless losses to Australia (2-0) and Paraguay (1-0), before a late 3-2 win over the USA that only avoided last place. It clicked too late.
URU:
## Read
Marcelo Bielsa was at the center of one of the biggest disappointments: Uruguay didn't win in Group H and finished last among the third-place finishers, eliminated.
## Campaign
2-2 with Cape Verde, 1-1 with Saudi Arabia and 1-0 to Spain. Just 2 points; Maxi Araujo, with 2 goals, was the only bright spot in a campaign to forget.
USA:
## Read
Mauricio Pochettino took the host nation to the top of Group D, even after stumbling in the final round.
## Campaign
4-1 over Paraguay (two from Balogun), 2-0 over Australia and a 3-2 loss to Turkey after already qualifying. A productive attack, but the defense left doubts for the knockout rounds.
UZB:
## Read
Fabio Cannavaro made Uzbekistan's World Cup debut, but the team bowed out in the Group Stage with the defense in tatters.
## Campaign
3-1 to Colombia, 5-0 to Portugal and a 3-1 comeback loss to DR Congo. Eleven goals conceded — hard lessons in the first appearance.
